title: 9 Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually
body_text:
9	Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually
9.1	Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually
Some remittance vouchers arenât available electronically. This section provides procedures for processing these vouchers manually. Front-line staff must complete the procedures below on the same day that you receive the CRA remittance voucher from your customer. Proceed as follows once youâve identified the form number and corresponding tax program, verified that all form fields are complete, and confirmed that the voucher isnât available in Business Taxes:
Debit the memberâs account for the amount on the remittance voucher and then credit the appropriate internal GL account for the same amount.
Stamp the remittance voucher and the stub with your financial institutionâs date stamp in the designated areas.
Separate the remittance voucher from its stub and give the stub to your customer.
Group remittance vouchers into a bundle with a maximum of 25 vouchers. Create additional bundles if necessary.
Create a tape listing of the remittance vouchers in the bundle. Ensure that each remittance voucher is included on the tape listing. Keep the order of the remittance vouchers in the same sequence as the tape listing.
Stamp the tape listing with your financial institutionâs identification stamp.
Prepare a draft for the grand total of the remittance voucher bundle and make it payable to the Receiver General.
Photocopy the remittance vouchers, tape listing, and the draft.
Bundle the remittance vouchers, tape listing, and the draft.
Store the photocopies in a secure location for up to 12 months (recommended).
Important
:	Ensure that the date recorded or stamped on the remittance voucher and its photocopy is clear and legible.
Mail or courier the bundle of remittance vouchers with the tape listing and the draft to:
Canada Revenue Agency
875 Heron Road
Ottawa, Ontario K1A 1B1
END
